content: Young restaurateurs are creating community spaces with run clubs, coffee raves and workshops. Here, lingering is encouraged, coffee mornings give way to cocktail nights, and globally inspired menus define Pune’s new slow-living café culture.

title: Waterlogging, jams & destruction: 10 videos capture Monsoon chaos across India
content: Heavy monsoon rains have caused widespread waterlogging and traffic disruptions across India. Several states are under red and orange alerts for severe weather conditions. Authorities are managing rescue operations and monitoring vulnerable areas. Maharashtra and Kerala continue to battle significant rainfall impacts. The India Meteorological Department forecasts continued heavy showers in many regions.

title: 'Days, weeks or months': US gearing up for prolonged military campaign against Iran
content: The United States anticipates a prolonged military confrontation with Iran lasting weeks. This escalation follows fresh US strikes and Iran's retaliatory actions in the region. Hardline factions in Iran are reportedly frustrated with the perceived lack of benefits from a memorandum of understanding. President Donald Trump declared a ceasefire over after Iranian attacks on commercial shipping.

title: 'Sack him...': Ben Stokes' post sparks fresh debate as ICC probes ECB over footage
content: Ben Stokes posted "Sack him..." on social media, reigniting retirement speculation.  The International Cricket Council is investigating the England and Wales Cricket Board over footage.  This footage was filmed inside the dressing room before Stokes announced his retirement.  The ICC alleges the ECB breached regulations by releasing the video early.  The investigation occurs as the ECB searches for a new Test captain.

title: Co-founder of $8 bn asset-management firm fired for not working from office 5 days a week
content: A co-founder is suing his former firm for thirty million dollars after his termination. He claims his partners used a return-to-office policy as an excuse. This policy was implemented to force employees back into the office full-time. Nieporte believed the mandate did not apply to him as a co-owner. He alleges his partners fabricated claims to strip him of his ownership stake.

title: Rashid Khan breaks down as Afghanistan bids emotional farewell to Shapoor Zadran
content: Afghanistan cricket bid an emotional farewell to former fast bowler Shapoor Zadran.  He passed away in India at the age of 38 after a prolonged illness.  Zadran represented Afghanistan in 44 ODIs and 36 T20Is between 2009 and 2020.  He had been undergoing treatment in New Delhi for a rare life-threatening condition.  His contributions and fighting spirit will remain an important part of cricket history.

title: Stir in Mumbai's political circles as Sharad Pawar meets Eknath Shinde
content: NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar met deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde, creating political stir.  Arif Naseem Khan also held a closed-door meeting with Pawar to gauge his political mood.  Rumors of Pawar's party joining the Mahayuti are circulating among Congress insiders.  Congress is working to reclaim political space if Pawar's party shifts alliances.  Pawar's party remains a constituent of the INDIA alliance, according to Congress.

title: New USCIS rules for H-1B, Green Card applicants start July 10
content: New United States Citizenship and Immigration Services rules will take effect soon. Applicants must use handwritten or properly reproduced signatures for immigration forms. Improper signatures will lead to rejection without any chance for correction. Applicants should always sign forms by hand before submitting them. This policy aims to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.

title: Exclusive: From Rajasthan to Texas - Abhimanyu Lamba's pursuit of the American dream
content: Texas Super Kings pacer Abhimanyu Lamba opens up on leaving Rajasthan to pursue cricket in the USA, overcoming homesickness, working as a part-time coach, and earning his place in the Major League Cricket (MLC). He also discussed cricket's rapid growth in America and his dream of one day representing the USA.

title: Mother who blamed vaccines for twins’ deaths now accused of murdering them
content: An Idaho woman faces murder charges for allegedly suffocating her infant twins. Andrea Shaw had previously claimed routine childhood vaccines caused their deaths. Prosecutors allege she deliberately killed Dallas and Tyson in May 2025. Shaw was a prominent figure in the anti-vaccine movement following the tragedy. Her defense plans to argue vaccine complications while she awaits trial.

title: Reflecting pool fix Trump said would cost under $2M ended at $14.7M
content: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation faces legal challenges and accusations of vandalism. Problems like algae and peeling paint emerged shortly after the project's completion. The White House blamed vandals, while critics pointed to a rushed renovation process. Criminal charges have been filed against individuals accused of damaging the landmark. A lawsuit also challenges the renovation's compliance with preservation and environmental laws

title: Nvidia partner Groq’s founder says his mistakes delayed the company by up to 4 years
content: Groq founder Jonathan Ross acknowledged his leadership errors significantly hindered company progress. He stated his initial management style cost Groq three to four years of development. Ross changed his hiring strategy to focus on selecting talent instead of growing it. A significant deal allowed Groq to remain independent while key personnel joined Nvidia. Other founders have also shared similar experiences regarding management challenges.

title: 'Decisions as swift as T20 match': PM Modi bats for deeper India-Australia ties
content: Prime Minister Modi highlighted India-Australia ties using cricket analogies during his visit. He stated that cricket serves as a diplomatic language between the two nations. Both countries will host major sporting events in the coming years. Terrorist attacks in Pahalgam and Sydney were strongly condemned by both leaders. A landmark atomic cooperation agreement was also announced by Prime Minister Modi.

title: Vienna's housing playbook: What rents look like inside Austria's capital
content: Vienna maintains affordable housing through a century-old social model. This system provides homes for half the city's population. Middle-income residents are included, preventing social segregation. Tenants enjoy open-ended contracts and no upfront deposit fees. The city retains public ownership and continues expanding supply.

title: Hack of the Day: How to secure your online accounts with passkeys
content: Passkeys offer a modern and secure alternative to traditional passwords for digital accounts.  These keys use your device's biometrics or screen lock for authentication.  Setting up passkeys requires compatible hardware and browsers for enhanced security.  Logging into accounts becomes a quick process using your fingerprint or face scan.  This method significantly reduces the risk of phishing attacks and password theft.

title: Chilling new images reveal condition of Connor Murphy’s Thailand home before drowning death
content: Connor Murphy died in Thailand after reportedly fleeing police and entering a lake, where he later drowned. Newly released images from his rental home show trash, medication packets and damage inside the property. Police are still investigating the case and awaiting autopsy and toxicology results. Authorities have not confirmed what led to Murphy’s behavior before his death.

title: NHL trade rumor: Penguins, Flyers eye Canucks' $92M star
content: The Vancouver Canucks have been actively looking for trade options for the team’s star center Elias Pettersson as the team rebuilds its roster, ahead of the new season. Elias Pettersson’s performance has also dropped in the last few years which has sparked some concern. Amid all the mess, it seems the Pittsburgh Penguins and the Philadelphia Flyers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Blake Lively didn't expect this from Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce: Report
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as 1000 high profile guests belonging to Hollywood and the sports world lined up to watch the pop star walk down the aisle, sparking chaos among milllions. While the wedding had brought New York City to a standstill, it seems Blake Lively might not have been that disappointed with not being invited.

title: 'Egypt were robbed': NYC mayor Mamdani joins VAR row over Argentina's dramatic comeback
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani voiced support for Egypt after their World Cup loss. He stated that the African team was "robbed" during their match against Argentina. This remark quickly gained attention amid ongoing officiating debates. Egypt's players and fans also alleged unfair refereeing decisions favoured Argentina. FIFA's head of refereeing defended the VAR interventions and warned against unfounded claims.

title: Exclusive: India gets its 98th GM! The making of teenage chess sensation Aswath S
content: India's 98th Grandmaster, Aswath S, achieved the coveted title after securing his final GM norm at the Pune International GM Round Robin 2026. The Tamil Nadu teenager overcame a four-month break for his Class 10 board exams, drawing inspiration from his family of chess coaches and mentors, and now has his sights set on breaking into the world's elite.

title: India’s holdings of US treasuries fall to near 6-year low — shift in RBI strategy?
content: The strategic importance of gold came into sharper focus in 2022 after Western sanctions led to the freezing of a substantial portion of Russia's foreign exchange reserves. The episode underscored gold's value as a reserve asset.

title: Another paper leak? NTA probes claims 90 UGC-NET sociology questions matched leaked PDF
content: The National Testing Agency faces new allegations of a paper leak for the UGC-NET exam. This follows recent issues with the NEET-UG exam and errors in other NET papers. Education ministry seeks details on a circulated PDF allegedly linked to the sociology paper. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi highlighted these serious allegations on social media. The controversy adds to existing concerns about high-stakes examination integrity.

title: 'He will be one of the best captains of India': Shresta backs brother Shreyas Iyer
content: Shreyas Iyer's sister has supported him after recent T20I losses. She believes he will become one of India's greatest captains. India suffered their biggest T20I defeat by runs against England. Iyer's captaincy has seen one win and ten defeats in twelve matches. India must win the next match to keep the series alive.

title: India-Australia-Canada form tech triangle: New pact to boost AI, critical minerals & more
content: India, Australia, and Canada will enhance technology and innovation cooperation through a new partnership. This pact aims to boost collaboration in critical and emerging technologies for mutual benefit. The agreement will focus on green energy, resilient supply chains, and critical minerals development. Leaders also discussed deepening defense, security, and economic ties between India and Australia. Future meetings will advance these collaborative efforts and explore new areas of partnership.

title: Watch: Bridge, airport & Chabahar hit by US strikes - 10 videos show devastation
content: The United States conducted airstrikes on approximately ninety Iranian military targets early Thursday. These strikes targeted air defense systems, missile sites, and naval infrastructure. Iran responded by launching missiles and drones at American military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait. President Trump declared the interim ceasefire with Iran effectively over following these events.

title: PM's Australia visit cements defence axis; P‑8s and joint drills ready Indo‑Pacific response
content: Prime Minister Modi's visit strengthens India-Australia strategic ties and regional security. Both nations are enhancing maritime security through joint patrols and information sharing. Military exercises like Pitch Black and Talisman Sabre will boost interoperability. Defence cooperation extends to secure communications and industrial partnerships. This collaboration solidifies their role as Indo-Pacific security providers.

title: Watch: Heavy rain sends 3,000 LPG cylinders floating down river in Maharashtra's Raigad
content: Heavy rain caused flooding in Raigad's Rasayani industrial area on Wednesday. Around three thousand LPG cylinders were swept into the Patalganga river. Authorities have retrieved approximately one thousand of these lost cylinders. The district administration requested residents to return any found cylinders. Sudden flooding was identified as the cause of this incident.

title: Will gold head to Rs 1.5 lakh/10 gm & silver to Rs 2.54 lakh per kg? Check outlook
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Both gold and silver are consolidating within a range, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: Rohit Sharma reaches London early to begin preps for ODIs against England
content: Rohit Sharma is aware of the challenges of playing in these conditions and wants to be fully prepared before action gets underway. The 39-year-old enjoys playing in England and has a phenomenal ODI record. In the 27 matches, the right-hander has accumulated 1428 runs at an average of 64.9 and hit seven hundreds - the most by a visiting batter.

title: Can landlord still demand rent when natural disaster strikes your home?
content: Natural disasters can disrupt the living conditions of tenants, leading to significant challenges in housing stability. Under the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, tenants have the right to terminate leases if their residence is rendered uninhabitable. Moreover, the Model Tenancy Act, 2021, strengthens tenant rights following such incidents, ensuring landlords cannot demand rent for properties until they are fully repaired. These regulations ensure tenants are protected during tumultuous times.

title: This one book made Nikhil Kamath rethink what success really means
content: Nikhil Kamath, co-founder of Zerodha and one of India's youngest self-made billionaires, says one of his biggest life lessons came not from business but from reading. After reading The Denial of Death by Ernest Becker, he began viewing time as the most valuable resource. Kamath's recommended books, covering psychology, money, philosophy, biology and society, highlight how continuous learning and curiosity can shape decision-making, success and personal growth beyond formal education.

title: Why the Income Tax Department is opposing a same-sex couple's plea for spousal tax benefits
content: A same-sex couple challenges a tax law in Bombay High Court. They seek gift tax exemption currently given to married heterosexual couples. The Income Tax Department opposes the plea, calling it misconceived and technically invalid. The court has set July 30, 2026, for the next hearing. This case questions the tax law's discriminatory application to same-sex partners.

title: 'Selective exemptions, inconsistent': India exposes US hypocrisy on Section 301 trade law
content: India questions US plans for new tariffs linked to alleged forced labor practices. New Delhi argues the US approach contains inconsistencies and prefers bilateral trade negotiations. Proposed tariffs could increase costs for Indian exporters and American consumers alike. India maintains its constitutional framework prevents forced labor and refutes US claims. The US Trade Representative is considering additional tariffs on imports from sixty economies.

title: Balogun red-card row: FIFA prez Infantino faces IOC ethics complaint over Trump lobbying
content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino faces a formal ethics complaint to the IOC. This complaint stems from alleged breaches of political neutrality rules. The issue arose after Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension was overturned. US President Donald Trump publicly claimed credit for influencing FIFA's decision. FairSquare organisation lodged the complaint, citing Infantino's conduct and ties.

title: PM Modi's Australia visit: Early trade pact, big investment, clean energy and more
content: India and Australia are strengthening economic ties and bilateral cooperation. Prime Minister Modi invited Australian businesses to invest in India's expanding economy. AustralianSuper announced a significant new investment in India's infrastructure fund. Discussions covered opportunities in clean energy, critical minerals, and technology sectors. Both nations aim to expand cooperation in trade and emerging sectors.

title: Man slits wife's throat at Andhra hospital after luring her out of ward
content: A man killed his wife by slitting her throat at the Kadiri government hospital. The accused, Ganga Raju, had marital discord and had previously thrashed his wife. He convinced her to leave the hospital ward for a private facility. While walking into the verandah, he attacked her with a knife. Police apprehended Ganga Raju and registered a case following the incident.

title: FIFA WC: Paraguay senator Amarilla launches fresh attack on Mbappe, refuses to back down
content: Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla intensified her verbal attacks on Kylian Mbappe.  She accused the French captain of disrespecting Paraguay's goalkeeper after their match.  Amarilla refused to apologise for her earlier racist and dehumanising remarks.  The controversy has drawn condemnation from French and international bodies.  Mbappe remains focused on the World Cup despite the ongoing off-field dispute.

title: India's white-ball reboot goes bust: Captaincy, execution and adaptation under scrutiny
content: Following a troubling series of defeats, the Indian T20 cricket team faces a critical juncture. Team captain Shreyas Iyer has criticized the current batting efforts, labeling them unacceptable. In light of recent lineup changes, head coach Gautam Gambhir describes this phase as a vital 'reset.' The team must overcome difficulties in adjusting to international playstyles while shedding IPL habits for enhanced future performance.

title: ‘Ramayana’ trailer to launch on July 18: Venue, time, cast, and more details revealed
content: The much-awaited Ramayana trailer will launch next weekend on July 18. This grand event will take place at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi. Director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra will attend the launch. Ranbir Kapoor and Sai Pallavi will be present, portraying Ram and Sita. The film's first part releases in Diwali 2026.

title: Mother donates part of her liver to one twin, uncle saves other in rare double transplant
content: Two young twins from the Philippines underwent a rare twin liver transplant in Delhi. Their mother and maternal uncle donated portions of their livers to save them. Doctors successfully performed the hospital's first-ever pediatric twin liver transplant surgery. Both children are now healthy and recovering well after the complex procedure. This remarkable medical feat offered new lives to the young siblings.

title: Love, affair, wife's death: How two civil services aspirants ended up in murder probe
content: Police arrested a sub-inspector in connection with the death of Amrita Kumari, wife of a block development officer. Her brother alleged her husband killed her due to an extramarital relationship and dowry harassment. The couple met while preparing for civil services and later married, but their relationship reportedly soured. Amrita was found unconscious and later died, leading to her brother filing an FIR against her husband and his family.

content: Prof Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, have spoken to NDTV about the mass uprising against Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.

title: Democrat Graham Platner Out Of US Senate Race After Rape Allegation
content: Graham Platner denies the rape claim but suspends his campaign, leaving Maine Democrats with days to find a replacement to challenge Susan Collins.

title: Trump Tempers Fury To End NATO Summit On High Note
content: Trump shifted from bashing allies over Iran to praising NATO unity, while licensing Ukraine to build Patriot missiles and securing U.S. ties.

title: On Way To Temple, 25-Year-Old Indian Student Killed In Road Accident In US
content: According to her family, Atluri was on her way to a temple with her friends when the accident occurred. While their car was waiting at a traffic signal, it was reportedly hit by another speeding vehicle from behind.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 500 Points Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude futures were up 78 cents at $78.80 a barrel, while US WTI crude is at $74.26.

title: Trump Warns US Will Hit Iran '20 Times' Harder If Tehran Doesn't Halt Attacks
content: Trump reinforced that message when speaking with reporters aboard Air Force One, returning from a NATO summit in Turkey.

title: Western Europe Records Hottest June Amid Intense Heatwave
content: Western Europe recorded its hottest June ever, with a brutal heat dome triggering thousands of deaths across France, Spain and Belgium

title: Ebola Deaths In Democratic Republic Of Congo Surpasses 500
content: Suspected Ebola cases have emerged in Kisangani, a major DR Congo city, as the outbreak's death toll hits 600 and health teams race to contain spread

title: The Paradox Of Millennium City: Gurugram Waterlogged And Gridlocked
content: A 2023 survey revealed that a staggering 96% of daily commuters in Gurugram report spending "significantly more time in traffic" due to waterlogging, compared to 88% in Delhi and 66% in Noida.

title: Extreme Weather Patterns: Is Climate Change Writing India's Weather Map?
content: Experts note that the process of global warming and climate change is a massive phenomenon occurring over a long period of time.

title: Assam Man Rapes, Murders 8-Year-Old Girl In Forest, Locals Demand Justice
content: According to the locals, despite informing the police personnel of the Madanmohan Patrol Post - located barely 300 metres from the crime scene - about the incident, they failed to reach the spot immediately.

title: "Will Never Go Back": Doctor Assaulted By Sena Corporator Quits, Leaves Thane
content: The incident took place on July 6 at the Shastri Nagar Hospital, run by the Kalyan-Dombivli Municipal Corporation in Dombivli.

title: At 15, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Has Rs 7 Crore. But Do Minors Pay Income Tax?
content: As per tax experts, the Income Tax Act contains "clubbing provisions" that add a minor child's income to the income of the parent who earns more.

title: Congress Lake Protection Wing HYDRAA Faces Charges Of Selective Targeting
content: HYDRAA said the land is listed in survey records as "vacant land, tank submerged" and that no no-objection certificate was issued by the district administration.

title: Microsoft Layoffs: Here's How Much Money Ex-Employees Could Receive
content: Microsoft Layoffs: The latest cuts have hit Microsoft's commercial sales division and Xbox gaming business the hardest.
